pi_items.source: Downloaded and verified from official Wireshark wiki: https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/27707187aeb30df68e70c8fb9d614981/http.cap. File is 26KB PCAP format with 43 total packets.

Official Wireshark display filter documentation: https://www.wireshark.org/docs/dfref/h/http.html confirms 'http' is the correct protocol field name to filter for HTTP traffic. Tested with tshark -Y 'http' on actual http.cap file.

Standard PCAP naming convention for filtered output files. Task setup scripts expect output at /home/ga/Documents/captures/filtered_http.pcap (export_result.sh line 16). Filename follows convention of original_filename + filter descriptor.

privileged_info_summary: The http.cap file is a real, publicly available sample PCAP from the official Wireshark project (26KB, 43 total packets) located at https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/27707187aeb30df68e70c8fb9d614981/http.cap. The display filter 'http' is the official Wireshark syntax for filtering HTTP protocol traffic, as documented in the official Wireshark display filter reference. The expected output filename 'filtered_http.pcap' follows standard PCAP naming conventions for filtered capture files.
